title: Add a delay effect
summary: Chain .delay(), .delaytime(), and .delayfeedback() on any pattern to add an echo effect. Time in seconds, feedback 0–1.

title: Add reverb
summary: Use .room() for reverb amount (0–1) and .size() for the tail length. Combine with .lpf() to tame brightness.

title: Build a drum pattern
summary: Layer kick, snare, and hi-hats using $: labels. Use .bank() to pick a machine, * for repeats, ~ for rests.
code:
setcpm(120/4)

$: s("bd*2").bank("RolandTR909").gain(0.9)
$: s("~ sd ~ sd").bank("RolandTR909")
$: s("hh*8").bank("RolandTR909").gain(0.45)
$: s("~ ~ ~ oh").bank("RolandTR909").gain(0.3)

title: Make a chord progression
summary: Use progression() with single-quoted chord symbols. Add rhythm presets, bass layers, and style presets for instant arrangements.

title: Euclidean rhythms
summary: Use (k,n) in mini-notation for Euclidean patterns: k hits spread evenly across n steps. Great for polyrhythms.

title: Filter sweep with an LFO
summary: Use sine.range() to sweep .lpf() over time. .slow() controls sweep speed. .lpq() adds resonance.

title: Create stereo width
summary: Use .jux() to split L/R and transform one side, or .pan() with a signal for auto-panning.
code:
setcpm(110/4)

// .jux(rev) plays original left, reversed right:
$: note("c4 e4 g4 b4").s("triangle")
  .jux(rev)
  .room(0.3)

// .pan() with sine for smooth auto-pan:
$: note("c3 e3 g3").s("gm_vibraphone")
  .pan(sine.slow(4))
  .room(0.4).gain(0.5)

title: Add randomness and probability
summary: Use ? in mini-notation for random drops, .sometimes() for random transforms, and .rand() / .choose() for variation.

title: Chop and slice samples
summary: Use .chop() to granulate, .splice() for rearranging, and .begin()/.end() for precise sample playback.

title: Create evolving patterns
summary: Use .every(), .slow(), and <> alternation to make patterns that change over time without manual arrangement.
code:
setcpm(120/4)

// .every() applies a transform periodically:
$: s("bd sd bd sd")
  .every(4, x => x.fast(2))
  .every(3, x => x.speed(1.5))

// <> alternation changes each cycle:
$: note("<c3 e3 g3 b3>").s("gm_vibraphone")
  .room(0.4)

// .slow() stretches across multiple cycles:
$: note("c3 d3 e3 f3 g3 a3 b3 c4")
  .s("triangle")
  .slow(4)
  .room(0.3).gain(0.5)

title: Signal modulation (LFOs)
summary: Use sine, saw, tri, square, and perlin as modulation sources. Chain .range() to set bounds and .slow()/.fast() for speed.

title: Build a breakdown with .mask()
summary: Use .mask() with <> alternation to silence layers on specific cycles. Perfect for drop/build arrangements.
code:
setcpm(130/4)

// .mask("<1 1 1 0>") silences every 4th cycle:
drum: s("bd*2, ~ sd:1, hh*8")
  .bank("RolandTR909")
  .mask("<1 1 1 0>")

bass: note("c2 c2 eb2 g2").s("sawtooth")
  .lpf(600).decay(0.15).sustain(0)
  .mask("<1 1 1 0>")

// Pad plays through the breakdown for continuity:
pad: note("<c3 eb3 g3 bb3>").s("gm_pad_warm")
  .room(0.6).gain(0.4)
